summary: Russian Army aviation struck a Ukrainian UAV launch point in Kolodeznoe, Kharkiv region, using LMUR missiles. "Elka" anti-aircraft drones continued kinetic interceptions of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with operators noted for disciplined communication. Ukrainian forces eliminated Russian propagandist Evgeny Nikolayev ("Hayduk"), co-founder of the WarGonzo channel, in Donetsk region on March 10 via FPV drone strike. Russian operators in the "Sever" group used "Molniya" drones to destroy Ukrainian UAV control points and pursued survivors with FPV drones in Korenek, Sumy region. The "Rubikon" Center reported 11 targets destroyed on March 16, including Ukrainian personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, and a Gepard self-propelled anti-aircraft gun. Iranian-backed militias attacked US Victory Base near Baghdad with fiber-optic FPV drones. Ukrainian forces employed log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas," enhancing range and resistance to jamming. Ukrainian FPV pilots disrupted Russian positions before strikes, while a Russian soldier was killed in a bayonet charge against an FPV drone, and another was hit simultaneously by two Ukrainian FPV drones while hiding behind a tree.
